title: Meta launches its Muse Image image generator and sparks controversy for using Instagram photos
summary: The artificial intelligence tool generates content from Instagram accounts, which has drawn criticism over privacy and the unauthorized use of personal data.

title: Anthropic's AI agent Claude Cowork is now available on web and mobile
summary: The tool allows running tasks in the background from any platform to increase productivity.

title: Microsoft replaces OpenAI and Anthropic models with its own AI to reduce costs
summary: The company migrates its AI services to proprietary models in order to cut costs and reduce dependency on external providers.

title: Nvidia presents its next-generation Rigel CPU core and highlights its single-threaded performance for AI agents
summary: The company reveals details of its Vera CPU and confirms the integration of the Rigel core in the GCC compiler, showing its commitment to high-performance computing in artificial intelligence.

title: Lexar and its parent company Longsys report explosive sales growth and unveil new DDR5 memory modules with Chinese CXMT chips
summary: Longsys forecasts earnings of $1.5 billion in the first half of 2026, boosted by demand for its Lexar products, while launching THOR memory with domestic technology.

title: Petition to save PlayStation physical discs surpasses 200,000 signatures after Sony's announcement to stop manufacturing physical games
summary: Users mobilize against Sony's decision to eliminate physical media, and PlayStation responds with PS Plus discounts for those who cancel.

title: DeepSeek develops its own AI inference chip to compete with Nvidia, as Chinese companies abandon the American manufacturer
summary: A survey reveals that Chinese firms are migrating to local AI providers, and DeepSeek accelerates its plan to manufacture its own processors.

title: Meta tests always-on AI glasses that record all day, raising privacy concerns
summary: The device, a rival to the Nano Banana 2, continuously captures the environment, which raises serious concerns about constant surveillance.

title: DLSS Swapper creator warns that user-uploaded DLL files may contain malware
summary: The modding tool for swapping DLSS versions can be unsafe if files are downloaded from unofficial sources.

title: Valve releases Windows drivers for Steam hardware, but refuses to offer official support
summary: The drivers allow installing Windows on Steam Machines, although Valve warns that it does not provide technical support for this configuration.

title: Intel Nova Lake CPUs regain AVX-512 support and offer a major leap in multimedia performance
summary: Recent Linux kernel patches confirm that Nova Lake's P and E cores will be able to execute 512-bit instructions natively.

title: Microsoft tests a cloud recovery feature to repair Windows 11 PCs that do not boot
summary: The Cloud Rebuild tool would allow restoring the operating system from the cloud without needing physical media.

title: X (Twitter) launches an integrated video editor to encourage original content and avoid reposts
summary: The social network introduces features like subtitles and green screen so creators can edit videos directly on the platform.

title: A vulnerability in GitHub workflows could expose private repository data through automated agents
summary: The flaw allows a public issue to trick GitHub agents into leaking confidential information.

title: Future Nvidia RTX 50 Super GPUs leaked with 10-17% higher power consumption than original models
summary: Seasonic's power supply calculator shows these unannounced graphics cards, hinting at their launch.

title: Google sets August 12 as launch date for the Pixel 11 series
summary: Google's annual event to showcase its new smartphones will take place in mid-August.

title: China considers imposing export restrictions on its AI models, while the US has already limited access
summary: Europe watches closely as the major powers weigh control measures for advanced AI.

title: OpenAI delays launch of GPT-5.6 by order of the US government and its lead futurist leaves the company
summary: The company faces regulatory challenges and changes in its management team.

title: Discord admits that a flaw in its AI moderation banned accounts for harmless content
summary: The AI tool incorrectly classified benign images as violative.

title: US Space Force receives its first mobile electromagnetic beam weapon to incapacitate enemy satellites
summary: The 'Meadowlands' system can detect, deny, and degrade hostile space assets from the ground.
